experiment: what-happened-when readout through a frozen LLM (content-binding language capstone)
note: SPLIT result. WHAT (event identity) transfers to language and is SIGNIFICANT: cortex-ON 76% vs text-only-OFF 26% (chance 33%), every seed ON>OFF, paired p=0.0328. WHEN (elapsed time) does NOT transfer in the JOINT what+when readout (exact p=0.78, within-1 p=0.88), even though (a) the cortex encodes both (CPU decode: what 100%, when 1.31 steps; results/content_binding.json) and (b) the STANDALONE elapsed-time readout transfers (results/elapsed_time_llm.json: ON 55% vs 16%, p=0.033). Interpretation: through the frozen-LLM fusion the CATEGORICAL field (what) is read but the SCALAR field (when) is dropped when both must be reported jointly -- a categorical-vs-scalar readout boundary, sharpening 'single-item readouts transfer, pairwise comparison does not'.
